items.title: Wintertransfers Eredivisie seizoen 2025/26: alle clubs op een rij
items.summary: De transferperiode is weer losgebarsten. In dit overzicht van VI vind je alle afgeronde, door clubs bevestigde, wintertransfers van de achttien Eredivisie-clubs. Clubs in Nederland kunnen tot en met 3 februari spelers aantrekken .

items.title: Anderlecht rondt huurdeal af na technisch probleem, hoop voor Fenerbahçe
items.summary: In de buitenlandse topcompetities is Deadline Day voorbij, maar in enkele landen kunnen nog deals gesloten worden. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Dit waren de duurste deals op de internationale wintertransfermarkt
items.summary: Antoine Semenyo is met afstand de duurste speler gebleken die in de winter van club is gewisseld. VI zet de twintig grootste transfers (op basis van de data van Transfermarkt) van de winterse transfermarkt op een rij.

items.title: Infantino 'infantile' for wanting to lift Russia ban
items.summary: Ukraine's sports minister calls Fifa president Gianni Infantino "irresponsible" and "infantile" for saying it will look at lifting a ban on Russia.

items.title: Ajax en de pijnlijke zoektocht naar een nummer 6
items.summary: Vandaag is het deadlineday in Nederland! Jos Boesveld en Sjoerd Keizer bespreken in de nieuwe VI ZSM transfers uit binnen- en buitenland. In dit fragment aandacht voor Ajax en de zoektocht naar een controleur op het Amsterdamse middenveld.

items.title: Romero haalt uit naar Spurs: 'Ongelooflijk, maar waar en schandalig'
items.summary: Een statement op sociale media van Cristian Romero heeft stof doen opwaaien in Engeland. Enkele minuten na het verstrijken van de transferdeadline liet de verdediger van zich horen.

items.title: NFL have no plans for ICE immigration enforcement at Super Bowl
items.summary: The NFL have no plans for ICE immigration enforcement at this Sunday's Super Bowl.

items.title: Women’s FA Cup to have seeded teams and no draw after last 32 under radical proposals
items.summary: FA plans sent to clubs are subject to consultation FA determined to grow Cup’s ‘commercial potential’ The Football Association has proposed radical changes to the Women’s FA Cup, including seeding four teams and dispensing with a draw after the last 32. The plans would introduce a “road-to-Wembley bracket” from the last 32, which would map out those teams’ route to the final, in the style of a World Cup. Continue reading...

items.title: Transfer window winners and losers: How Man City, Liverpool, PSG, others fared
items.summary: Deadline day was extremely quiet and the winter transfer window as a whole had its share of perplexing player moves. Here are our winners and losers.

items.title: Men's grades: Al Hilal get a B for Benzema deal
items.summary: The 2026 winter transfer window is now closed. Here is how we rated the biggest deals.

items.title: Benzema joins Al Hilal as Kante heads to Fener
items.summary: Karim Benzema has left Al Ittihad and signed a contract with Saudi Pro League rivals Al Hilal, the club announced Monday.

items.title: Liverpool seal $74M deal for Jacquet from Rennes
items.summary: Liverpool have confirmed the signing of defender Jérémy Jacquet from Rennes who is set to join the club in the summer.

items.title: Sources: Ronaldo dispute with PIF behind absence
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo will miss Al Nassr's Saudi Pro League match with Al Riyadh as he is unhappy with PIF's (Saudi Arabian Public Investment Fund) lack of transfer activity at the club compared to other clubs owned by the fund.

items.title: 'Still some time' - Tiger provides update on golf return
items.summary: Tiger Woods says it will still be "some time" until he returns to professional golf as he watched his Jupiter Links side earn their first TGL win in 2026 on Monday.

items.title: Can Alcaraz complete the calendar Grand Slam? 'He can become the GOAT'
items.summary: "It's going to be a big challenge" - After winning his maiden Australian Open world No 1 Carlos Alcaraz is now targeting the calendar Grand Slam of all four majors in a single year? Sky Sports' Jonathan Overend believes he can become the GOAT!

items.title: What to look out for on WSL transfer deadline day
items.summary: The Women's Super League transfer window shuts on Tuesday - so what can we expect to happen on the final day of business?

items.title: Just seven signings - the Premier League's quiet deadline day
items.summary: A low-key transfer deadline day ended with the fewest Premier League deals completed in a winter window. BBC Sport looks at the spending that was done, and examines why there was so little activity.

items.title: West Ham convince Chelsea defender Axel Disasi to join on loan for season
items.summary: Disasi moving out as Sarr returns to Blues from loan Liverpool seal £60m Jacquet signing for next season West Ham are bolstering their defensive options by signing Axel Disasi on loan from Chelsea. The deal for the centre-back, who has not played since last April, will run until the end of the season and does not contain an option or obligation to buy. Nuno Espírito Santo has been looking to boost his side’s hopes of avoiding relegation from the Premier League by signing a defender. Disasi was a key target, although he took some convincing to agree to move to the London Stadium. Talks over personal terms dragged on, forcing West Ham to submit a deal sheet to give themselves extra time to ensure the loan went through. They have paid a £1.7m loan fee. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Strong and smart' - Liverpool's £60m man, who can 'become like Van Dijk'
items.summary: Jeremy Jacquet will join Liverpool for £60m from Rennes in the summer - but why are the Reds signing him?

items.title: Arteta calls Arsenal ‘most exciting team in Europe’ after Scholes’ criticism
items.summary: ‘We have the most goals, the most clean sheets’ Liam Rosenior predicts ‘physical’ Carabao Cup second leg Mikel Arteta has laughed off a suggestion from Paul Scholes that Arsenal would be the most boring team to win the Premier League, insisting his side are considered “the most exciting in Europe” in other countries. Scholes, the former Manchester United midfielder, pointed to the lack of goals from Arsenal’s front four this season and reliance on set pieces as evidence for his claim. Viktor Gyökeres is the club’s top scorer in the league with six, and Arsenal have scored 17 goals from set pieces – three more than any other club. Continue reading...
